story.iWant: to provide high-level feedback ("too corporate", "colors off") and see updated samples
story.soThat: the theme matches my vision without micro-managing details

subtasks.subtask: 1.1: Create feedback parsing logic to identify feedback type
1.2: Map gestalt terms to theme adjustments per tech spec table
1.3: Handle ambiguous feedback by asking clarifying questions
1.4: Generate theme modification instructions from parsed feedback
2.1: Apply feedback-derived modifications to theme.json
2.2: Recalculate dependent values (e.g., text colors for contrast)
2.3: Preserve unaffected theme sections
2.4: Save updated theme.json to .slide-builder/theme.json
3.1: Re-invoke frontend-design skill for all 6 slides with updated theme
3.2: Follow same sample generation flow as Story 2.4
3.3: Overwrite existing samples with regenerated versions
3.4: Report progress during regeneration
4.1: Track feedback iteration count in status.yaml
4.2: After 3 iterations, check if user still not satisfied
4.3: Offer direct theme.json editing as escape hatch
4.4: Document escape hatch path in completion message
5.1: On approval, create .slide-builder/templates/ directory
5.2: Copy sample files to templates with naming conversion
5.3: Update theme.json layouts section with file references
5.4: Create .slide-builder/theme-history/ directory if not exists
5.5: Save theme snapshot to theme-history/theme-v1-{date}.json
6.1: Expand Phase 5 placeholder in setup/instructions.md
6.2: Add feedback loop with iteration counter
6.3: Add action to interpret feedback and modify theme
6.4: Add action to regenerate samples
6.5: Add check for 3+ iterations and escape hatch offer
6.6: Add finalization actions (copy to templates, save history)
6.7: Update status.yaml with phase completion
7.1: Test feedback "make it bolder" - verify higher contrast
7.2: Test feedback "too corporate" - verify softer tones
7.3: Test approval flow - verify templates created
7.4: Test 4 iterations - verify escape hatch offered
7.5: Verify theme-history entry created with timestamp
7.6: Verify theme.json layouts section updated

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Given the sample deck is displayed, when the user provides gestalt feedback (e.g., "too corporate", "colors off"), then the system interprets the feedback and adjusts theme primitives
The system regenerates the sample deck with updated theme for re-validation
Given the user approves the samples ("Perfect"), when they confirm approval, then the theme is locked (saved to theme.json)
Upon approval, sample slides are copied to templates/ as layout templates (layout-title.html, layout-list.html, etc.)
A version entry is created in theme-history/ with timestamp
Given feedback exceeds 3 rounds, then direct theme.json editing is offered as escape hatch

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Defines acceptance criteria, feedback interpretation mapping table, and Phase 5 workflow sequence for the feedback loop.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Specifies Feedback Interpreter module (parses natural language, maps to theme adjustments) and Template Finalizer module (copies samples to templates, saves theme history).

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Defines .slide-builder/ directory structure including templates/ and theme-history/ subdirectories for layout storage and version snapshots.

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Templates generated during setup are reused during build phase. Sample slides become layout templates after user approval.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Users provide high-level feedback on sample deck, system interprets gestalt feedback and updates theme, regenerates sample deck for re-validation.

doc.title: Epics and Stories
doc.snippet: User story definition and high-level acceptance criteria for feedback loop implementation.

doc.title: Story 2.4: Sample Deck Generation
doc.snippet: Previous story learnings: Sample naming uses 01-title.html pattern, all 6 slides exist in .slide-builder/samples/, CSS variables pattern established.

file.reason: Phase 5 feedback loop placeholder needs expansion with full feedback interpretation, iteration tracking, and finalization logic
Main workflow configuration that orchestrates the setup process including the feedback loop phase
Theme file that will be modified based on feedback. Contains colors, typography, shapes, layouts, and personality sections.
Status file needs iteration tracking field added for feedback loop count
Sample slide that will be regenerated with theme updates and copied to templates/ on approval
Sample slide for list/agenda layout, regenerated during feedback loop
Sample slide for process flow layout, regenerated during feedback loop
Sample slide for comparison layout, regenerated during feedback loop
Sample slide for callout/insight layout, regenerated during feedback loop
Sample slide for code/technical layout, regenerated during feedback loop

node.usage: Headless browser for HTML-to-image conversion during export
Google Slides API integration for export workflow

constraints.constraint: Per ADR-002: Templates generated during setup are reused during build phase - do not create new template patterns
theme-history/ directory stores version snapshots with timestamp naming: theme-v{n}-{date}.json
Per NFR5: Theme feedback loop iteration should "feel responsive" - minimize wait time between feedback and regeneration
Per NFR15: Theme changes must not corrupt existing slides - preserve structure while updating values
Target 1-3 feedback rounds before approval - offer escape hatch after 3 iterations
Use frontend-design skill for slide regeneration - same pattern as Story 2.4 Phase 4
Template naming convention: 01-title.html → layout-title.html (use actual sample filenames, not spec'd sample-1-* pattern)
All workflow instructions must follow BMAD workflow.xml execution patterns including step/action/check tags

interface.name: theme.json schema
interface.signature: { meta: {name, version, created, sources, locked, approved}, colors: {primary, secondary, accent, background: {default, alt}, text: {heading, body}, semantic: {...}}, typography: {fonts: {heading, body, mono}, scale: {hero, h1, h2, h3, body, small}, weights: {...}}, shapes: {boxes: {...}, arrows: {...}, lines: {...}}, layouts: {...}, personality: {classification, confidence, notes} }

interface.name: status.yaml schema
interface.signature: { mode, phase, workflow_version, assets: {...}, extraction: {...}, theme: {file, status, personality, confidence, approved}, templates: {directory, count, files: []}, last_action, last_modified, history: [] }

interface.name: Feedback Interpretation Mapping
interface.signature: "too corporate" → softer colors (+10% saturation reduction), larger corner radius (+4px), warmer text colors "not bold enough" → higher contrast (darken primary by 15%), reduce corner radius (-4px), increase font weights "colors off" → re-weight extraction sources, ask clarifying question "too busy" → increase whitespace (larger margins), reduce shadow intensity "too plain" → add accent color usage, increase shadow depth "fonts don't feel right" → re-analyze sources, ask for preference

interface.name: Sample to Template Filename Mapping
interface.signature: 01-title.html → layout-title.html 02-agenda.html → layout-list.html 03-flow.html → layout-flow.html 04-comparison.html → layout-columns-2.html 05-callout.html → layout-callout.html 06-technical.html → layout-code.html

interface.name: frontend-design skill invocation
interface.signature: Skill(skill="frontend-design") - loads professional design expertise for slide generation

tests.standards:
This project uses BMAD workflow-based validation. Testing is primarily manual via the Frontend Test Gate pattern: 1. Prerequisites verification (prior story completion, file existence) 2. Manual browser testing following test steps table 3. Success criteria validation with checklist 4. Feedback questions for qualitative assessment
No automated test framework is currently configured (package.json shows "no test specified"). Testing validates workflow execution and visual output quality.

ideas.idea: Test feedback "make it bolder" - verify theme.json shows higher contrast values (darker primary, heavier weights)
Test feedback "too corporate" - verify theme.json shows softer colors, larger corner radius
Test feedback "colors off" - verify system asks clarifying question before adjusting
Verify all 6 sample slides regenerate with updated CSS variables after theme modification
Verify regeneration preserves slide structure while updating visual styling
Test approval with "Perfect" response - verify theme.json meta.locked = true and meta.approved set
Verify .slide-builder/templates/ directory created with 6 layout files using correct naming
Verify template content matches final sample content (CSS variables, structure)
Verify .slide-builder/theme-history/theme-v1-{date}.json created with full theme snapshot
Test 4 feedback iterations - verify escape hatch message offered for direct JSON editing
Verify iteration count tracked in status.yaml during feedback loop
